trajectory.description: Observation: Iter1 ResNet50 baseline gets balanced_accuracy=0.363 on L100 (vs val_acc=0.90), with 0.0 on bird/squirrel and low on several others. Analysis: Severe domain shift (backgrounds/lighting/species diffs); lacks aug, class balancing, robust backbone, and sufficient training for generalization. CLIP-ViT likely helps due to diverse pretraining. Decision: Switch to timm vit_base_patch16_clip_224.openai (86M params), add RandCrop/Flip/Jitter + CLIP norm in train, CenterCrop in val/test, class-weighted loss, AdamW+cosine scheduler, 10 epochs, best-val checkpointing, improved logging. Submit eval immediately after to track progress within budgets (~5min/iter est.).

Training curve shows steady val improvement. Analysis: Domain shift severe; class imbalance not fully mitigated by weights alone, augments insufficient for generalization. Decision: Switch to WeightedRandomSampler + label smoothing + no class-weighted loss, add RandAugment for stronger regularization, use val balanced_accuracy for best checkpoint selection (via sklearn), lower LR=1e-5, 20 epochs, batch=64. Submit eval to track. This targets rare classes and robustness within time (~20min/iter).

Best remains 0.5657 from CLIP-ViT iter2. Analysis: Overfitting to source domains despite augs; rare class imbalance and domain shift (lighting/backgrounds) not fully addressed by current regularization. Decision: Incrementally add Mixup (alpha=0.2) for stronger generalization/regularization, combine with weighted CE loss, slightly higher LR=3e-5 with linear warmup, keep 20 epochs and best-val_bal checkpointing. Update predict.py with simple TTA (original + hflip averaged logits) to boost inference without extra training cost. This targets rare classes and robustness; submit_eval after to measure within ~1.5h remaining budget.

Curves indicate steady val improvement but clear source-target gap. Analysis: Mixup hurt some classes despite helping others; CLIP-ViT good but needs better regularization for DG without hurting rare class consistency. Decision: Revert to no-mixup/no-weighted-loss (like successful iter3), add drop_rate=0.1, stronger RandAugment (ops=3,mag=15)+ColorJitter, lower LR=1e-5+higher WD=0.05 for less overfitting, keep sampler+label_smoothing+val_bal_acc selection+20 epochs+TTA. Update model.py for drop_rate. This should stabilize rare classes; submit_eval to track within ~1.2h budget.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best bal_acc still 0.5657 (iter2 CLIP-ViT); recent runs hit val_bal~0.87 but test ~0.55 with high variance on rare classes (bird 0.07-0.85, squirrel 0.03-0.12). Focal loss should upweight hard examples (often rare/shifted), extra camera-specific augs (blur/grayscale) improve robustness without hurting common classes. Decision: Add FocalLoss(gamma=2.0), GaussianBlur+RandomGrayscale to train transforms, drop_rate=0.15, epochs=15 (to fit ~57min remaining), remove label_smoothing; keep sampler+RandAugment+val_bal selection+TTA. Submit eval immediately after training to measure improvement within time/iter budget.
